name: Route of the Dolmens and Castles of Granucillo
name_original: Granucillo desde Brime de Urz — circular por las Dolmans y el Castillo
description: Hike a 9.6-mile moderate loop from Brime de Urz, exploring prehistoric dolmens and the medieval Castle-Palace of the Counts of Benavente.
region_intro: Hiking around Brime De Urz offers diverse landscapes within Spain's Zamora province, characterized by hills and the serene Almucera river. The region features expansive vineyards and historical attractions. Trails explore varied terrain, from riverbanks to areas with significant elevation changes, providing opportunities to experience rural Spain. Best hiking trails around Brime De Urz The most popular hiking route is Basilia's Balcony and Coto, a 4.9 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 13 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the Coto area, offering views of the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Banks of the Eria and Órbigo Trail, a moderate 8.3 miles (13.4 km) path. This trail follows the banks of the Eria and Órbigo rivers, featuring minimal elevation gain. Local hikers also love the Peña Redonda and miradores del Coto and de las Suertes — circular by the Valles de Benavente from Morales de Rey, a 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail leading through the Valles de Benavente, often completed in about 3 hours 43 minutes. Hiking around Brime De Urz is defined by hills, river valleys, and vineyards, with historical landmarks integrated into the routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to longer treks. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 10 hikers have used komoot to explore Brime De Urz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Brime de Urz?
A: Brime de Urz, located in Spain's Zamora province, offers diverse landscapes characterized by rolling hills, serene natural areas, and the Almucera river. You'll also encounter expansive vineyards, which are part of the Valles de Benavente denomination, adding to the scenic beauty.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Brime de Urz?
A: Yes, Brime de Urz offers trails suitable for various skill levels. Among the 10 available routes, 3 are classified as easy, making them ideal for beginners or families looking for a relaxed walk. These routes generally feature minimal elevation gain and cover shorter distances.

Q: What historical landmarks or points of interest can I discover on the trails?
A: The hiking trails in Brime de Urz are rich with historical and cultural points of interest. You can explore ancient sites like the Dolmen de Piedra Fincada, an Iron Age 'castro' (hillfort), and a Roman camp. The Romanesque Hermitage of San Esteban, perched on a hill, offers great views. Additionally, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4092040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Vizana&lt;/a&gt;, a Roman bridge over the Órbigo River, is another significant attraction.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022509543/pena-redonda-and-miradores-del-coto-and-de-las-suertes-circular-by-the-valles-de-benavente-from-morales-de-rey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Redonda and miradores del Coto and de las Suertes — circular by the Valles de Benavente from Morales de Rey&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option that takes you through the scenic Valles de Benavente. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305549898/arrabalde-circular-through-the-sierra-de-las-labradas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrabalde — circular through the Sierra de las Labradas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I hike along rivers or discover natural water features?
A: Absolutely. The Almucera river flows through the region, and you can find trails that follow its banks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022487025/banks-of-the-eria-and-orbigo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Banks of the Eria and Órbigo Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route specifically designed to explore the natural beauty along these rivers, featuring minimal elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Brime de Urz?
A: The region's diverse landscapes, including river valleys and vineyards, are enjoyable throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for hiking, with vibrant natural colors. Summer can be warm, but riverbank trails provide cooler options.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Brime de Urz?
A: While specific regulations for each trail can vary, generally, dogs are welcome on many outdoor trails in rural Spain, especially if kept on a leash and waste is properly disposed of. It's always advisable to check local signage or specific route details for any restrictions before heading out with your dog.

Q: What are some notable castles or ancient structures I can visit near Brime de Urz?
A: Beyond the Dolmen de Piedra Fincada, the area is home to several historical castles. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5453127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granucillo Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de los Pimentel&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305829771/route-of-the-dolmens-and-castles-of-granucillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Dolmens and Castles of Granucillo&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights these ancient structures.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in the Brime de Urz area?
A: There are 10 hiking routes available in the Brime de Urz area, offering a variety of distances and difficulties to suit different preferences.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Brime de Urz?
A: More than 10 hikers have used komoot to explore Brime de Urz's varied terrain. They often praise the region for its blend of natural beauty, including hills, river valleys, and vineyards, alongside integrated historical landmarks, providing a rich and engaging hiking experience.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach the hiking trailheads?
A: Public transport options to specific trailheads in rural areas like Brime de Urz can be limited. It is generally recommended to use a personal vehicle to access the starting points of the hiking routes, as this offers the most flexibility. Parking is usually available in or near the villages.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic vistas?
A: Yes, the region's hilly terrain provides several opportunities for scenic viewpoints. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022509543/pena-redonda-and-miradores-del-coto-and-de-las-suertes-circular-by-the-valles-de-benavente-from-morales-de-rey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Redonda and miradores del Coto and de las Suertes&lt;/a&gt; route is known for its 'miradores' (viewpoints) that offer expansive views over the Valles de Benavente.
tips: Brime de Urz is a small town at the foot of the Sierra de Carpurias. Walk to the small chapel at the top of the hill where you can take in beautiful views of the valley and mountains.
A prehistoric stone circle. The structure was usually built from locally quarried granite or slate. They were used for various ceremonies throughout the seasons.
Surrounded by fields is a small and charming chapel on the outskirts of the town of Granucillo. You can rest under the shade of the trees during your walk.
Rectangular wall that surrounds the ruins of the Castle-palace of the counts of Benavente built in the 15th century.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Granucillo from Brime de Urz circular trail?
faqs.answer: This circular trail is classified as moderate. It features mostly easily accessible paths and a relatively flat or gently undulating path with around 90 meters of elevation gain over 15.4 kilometers, making it suitable for a wide range of hikers, including beginners.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily features easily accessible paths, suggesting a generally well-maintained surface. You'll traverse varied natural surfaces as the route winds through the Sierra de Carpurias, between the Vidriales Valley and the Eria River plain, often passing through agricultural fields.

faqs.question: What historical and natural sights can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in history and natural beauty. You'll encounter prehistoric megalithic structures like the Dolmen de San Adrián, which has been partially reconstructed. The trail also passes by the ruins of the medieval Castle-Palace of the Counts of Benavente in Granucillo, offering a glimpse into the region's past. Additionally, you'll see the Chapel of Brime de Urz and the Ermita de San Adrián, Granucillo.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a peaceful immersion in the rural landscapes of Zamora, making it enjoyable across multiple seasons. Given its moderate difficulty and accessible paths, spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for hiking. Summer can also be pleasant, though it might be warmer.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead for this circular hike?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details are not provided, as a circular route starting from Brime de Urz, it is generally expected that parking would be available in or near the village to begin the loop. Look for designated parking areas or suitable roadside spots in Brime de Urz.

faqs.question: Is the Granucillo from Brime de Urz circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally considered dog-friendly. Given its rural setting and accessible paths, it's a great option for hiking with your dog. Always ensure your dog is under control, preferably on a leash, and remember to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail. It is a publicly accessible route through the natural and historical landscapes of the region.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several other paths. You will find yourself on parts of the Camino de los Bacillares, Camino Zamorano, Camino Entremolinos, Camino de San Esteban, Camino de Valdezuelas, Calle Palacio, Camino de Santa Marta, Camino Bajo la Peña, Camino de Peñas Altas, and Camino de Monte el Conde.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restrooms or water sources along the trail?
faqs.answer: As a rural trail, dedicated restrooms or official water refill stations are unlikely to be found directly along the path. It's advisable to carry sufficient water for the entire 15.4 km duration and plan for facilities in the villages of Brime de Urz or Granucillo before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What should I pack for the Granucillo from Brime de Urz circular hike?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, we recommend packing comfortable hiking shoes, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a light jacket depending on the weather. A camera is also a good idea to capture the historical sites and rural scenery. Don't forget your phone for navigation and emergencies.

waypoints.name: Castle-Palace of the Counts of Benavente
